message.content: {"items":[{"title":"American Revolution, 1775-1783: A Short History (ICPSR 09955) – GESIS entry","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA9955","justification":"GESIS entry for ICPSR study on the American Revolution, directly covering late‑18th‑century American history including colonies such as Massachusetts and South Carolina (1770s–1780s)."},{"title":"Historical Statistics of the United States, Colonial Times to 1970 (GESIS Study)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8323","justification":"Provides quantitative historical data for the American colonies and early United States from colonial times through 1970, covering South Carolina and Massachusetts across the 1730s–1790s period."},{"title":"United States Historical Census Data (1790‑1960) – GESIS","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8168","justification":"Contains historical U.S. census information starting with the first federal census in 1790, giving demographic and structural context for South Carolina and Massachusetts in the 1790s."},{"title":"United States National Historical Geographic Information System (NHGIS) – GESIS description","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/en/research_data/ZA5847","justification":"GESIS catalog entry describing NHGIS, which supplies historical U.S. census and related data including early census years, allowing analysis of late‑18th‑century conditions in South Carolina and Massachusetts."},{"title":"Cross‑National Time‑Series Data Archive (CNTS) – GESIS","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/en/research_data/ZA8063","justification":"Long‑run historical time‑series for countries including the United States; useful for contextualizing political and conflict history around the Revolutionary era (1770s–1790s)."},{"title":"Correlates of War Project: Colonial/Dependency Contiguity Data – GESIS","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/en/research_data/ZA5324","justification":"Historical international relations dataset that includes colonial relationships; relevant for studying the British–American colonial structure that framed South Carolina and Massachusetts in the 18th century."},{"title":"World Religion Data, 1900‑2000 with Historical Notes – GESIS","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/en/research_data/ZA4853","justification":"While its quantitative coverage is 1900‑2000, it provides historical background notes on earlier religious distributions, including references to colonial American regions such as Massachusetts and South Carolina."},{"title":"U.S. Presidential Elections, 1789‑1992 – GESIS","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA2291","justification":"Contains data beginning with the first U.S. presidential election in 1789, providing political‑historical information for the new republic including state‑level patterns involving Massachusetts and South Carolina in the 1790s."},{"title":"Polity IV: Political Regime Characteristics and Transitions, 1800‑2004 – GESIS entry","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/en/research_data/ZA4799","justification":"Although time series start in 1800, the documentation and coding schemes draw on late‑18th‑century U.S. institutional history relevant to the period after the Revolution."},{"title":"International Conflict Behavior (ICB) Project – GESIS","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/en/research_data/ZA3039","justification":"Includes coded international and colonial conflict episodes; documentation touches on the American Revolutionary conflict involving the colonies including Massachusetts and South Carolina in the 1770s–1780s."}]}
